steps.title: Define the goal precisely
steps.content: The student wants to explain the sculpture's specific historical context. That means identifying the particular historical event the artwork relates to and its temporal connection — not describing the sculpture's appearance, quoting a critic's interpretation, or giving the artist's biography.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — According to art historian Kirsten Buick, Forever Free, which depicts a male figure and a female figure gazing upward, is a "celebration of liberty.": This cites a critic's general interpretation and describes what the sculpture looks like. Neither provides specific historical context. ✗
  • Choice B — The base of Edmonia Lewis's 1867 marble sculpture is inscribed with a historically significant phrase: "forever free.": This mentions a historically significant phrase but never explains what historical event it connects to or why it matters. ✗
  • Choice C — Completed in 1867, Lewis's sculpture Forever Free commemorates the Emancipation Proclamation, which had been issued four years previously.: This names the specific historical event (the Emancipation Proclamation, 1863) and establishes the temporal relationship to the sculpture (completed 1867, four years later). That is exactly the specific historical context requested. ✓
  • Choice D — Forever Free (1867) is a marble sculpture by Edmonia Lewis, an African American and Mississauga Ojibwe sculptor who lived from 1844 to 1907.: This gives biographical information about the artist, not historical context for the sculpture itself. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
